users.title: User management
users.subtitle: Create admins and guides for the selected organization.

guides.title: Guides
guides.subtitle: Manage guides in your organization.
guides.createHelp: This screen stays organization-scoped. If the email already belongs to a guide, the existing global guide account is attached to this organization instead of creating a duplicate account. In that case, the name and password fields do not overwrite the existing account.
guides.passwordHint: Name and password are used only when creating a brand-new guide. If the email already belongs to a guide, the entered name does not replace the existing account, and the password is ignored; the guide is only attached to this organization. The name is used only as a fallback when the existing guide record is empty.

helper.title: Template fill helper
helper.subtitle: Use these short rules to fill the template correctly and quickly.
helper.generalTemplateRule: Always start from the latest template downloaded from this page.
helper.generalLanguageRule: Headers support TR/EN; import maps them automatically.
helper.generalEmptyRowsRule: Empty rows are skipped; non-empty rows are validated against required fields.
helper.participantsCoreRule: participants sheet is for core participant data plus accommodation/insurance/transfer fields.
helper.participantsNameRule: In the new template, first_name and last_name are required for participant names.
helper.participantsNoFlightRule: In the new model, do not put flight columns in participants; use flight_segments sheet.
helper.participantsNoAccommodationRule: If you use accommodation_segments or accommodation_assignments, do not fill legacy accommodation columns on participants.
helper.participantsUpdateRule: If tc_no already exists in the same event, the participant is updated.
helper.segmentsRequiredRule: Each segment row represents one flight leg.
helper.segmentsParticipantNameRule: participant_name is optional and only for operator reference; it is not used for matching.
helper.segmentsDirectionRule: direction accepts Arrival/Return, and also Gidiş/Dönüş.
helper.segmentsIndexRule: segment_index should be ordered as 1..N per direction.
helper.segmentsDateTimeRule: Recommended date/time format: yyyy-MM-dd and HH:mm.
helper.segmentsBaggageRule: baggage_pieces must be whole number; baggage_total_kg decimals are rounded with a warning.
helper.segmentsCabinBaggageRule: cabin_baggage is free text, for example 8 kg, 1 cabin suitcase, or personal item.
helper.accommodationSegmentsRule: accommodation_segments creates or updates dated accommodation plans.
helper.accommodationAssignmentsRule: accommodation_assignments applies per-participant hotel overrides and room details.
helper.accommodationSegmentKeyRule: segment_key is file-local only; the assignments sheet uses it to target the right plan.
helper.accommodationResolveRule: accommodation and accommodation_override resolve an existing accommodation by tab id or title.
helper.accommodationRequiredColumnsValue:
accommodation_segments: segment_key, accommodation, start_date, end_date
accommodation_assignments: tc_no, segment_key
helper.requiredColumnsValue: tc_no, direction, segment_index, departure_airport, arrival_airport, flight_code, departure_date, departure_time
helper.issueDirectionRule: If direction is invalid, use Arrival/Return (or Gidiş/Dönüş).
helper.issueNotFoundRule: tc_no not found means this identity number is not in the selected event.
helper.issueParticipantNameMismatchRule: participant_name is only for verification. A mismatch creates a warning, but matching still uses tc_no.
helper.issueLegacyIgnoredRule: If flight_segments has rows, legacy flight fields in participants are ignored (warning).
helper.issueAccommodationLegacyIgnoredRule: If accommodation sheets have rows, legacy accommodation fields in participants are ignored (warning).
helper.issuePreviewLimitRule: Preview shows first 200 rows; apply processes the full file.
helper.tipTwoByTwoRule: For connection examples, add 2 arrival rows (segment 1-2) and 2 return rows (segment 1-2) for the same TC.
helper.tipGroupByTcRule: For readability, keep rows grouped by the same tc_no.
